adj. & s.m. Enjoying; using, possessing; suffering, experiencing; full of enjoyments; devoted to enjoyments, jovial, jolly; indulging in sensual pleasures; — one who enjoys or possesses (a thing, &c.), one who makes mere pleasure a pursuit, man of pleasure, one given up to carnal pleasures, voluptuary, rake, debauchee; — king, prince; head-man of a village; barber.
more: n. m. 1. A man of pleasure; voluptuary; Sybarite. Man bhogī, karam diladdrī . Prov. For pleasure made, to poverty foredoomed. 2. One given up to carnal pleasures ( kāmī ); a rake; debauchee. Bhogī/?/so rogī . Prov. On Sybarite diseases light. 3. ( In law ). One who enjoys the usufruct of a property.
adj. Jovial, jolly. من بهوگي کرم دردري man bhogī karm daridrī, By nature fond of pleasure, but condemned by fate to poverty.

v.t. To enjoy; to experience, suffer, undergo; to be requited for, to taste; to endure, bear, put up with; to take or receive pleasure or pain with indifference, to live stoically.
more: v. n. 1. To feel or experience pleasure; enjoy; relish; revel. 2. To bear; suffer; endure (pain). 1. Chakkī dharī agāṛī mere jab se mujhe biyāhī, 'E bhaṛve! maiṅ tere ān-ke bhogī sadā tavāī .' Play. The very day he wed me he a flour-mill set 'fore me, 'Thou wh—eson! in thy house I've suffered constant misery.' 2. E beṭī rī, janam ke sāthī haiṅ māī aur bāp, Dukh sukh apne karam kā bhogeṅ apne āp . Play. My daughter! at your birth your father, mother, both attend, The good and evil in your fate on you alone impend. 3. To take pain and pleasure as they come.
v. n. To enjoy, to suffer.

more: v. n. S. भष् 1. To bark (a dog). 1. Adab, chal dūr ho, kyā bhauṅktā hai tū mūë gurjī? Mujhe nafrat hai, maiṅ kuttā na pālūṅ terī raṅgat kā . Wom. Rāhat. 2. Kuttā dekhegā na bhauṅkegā . Prov. If the dog sees it, he will bark. (Keep it out of sight, or somebody will want it). 3. Kuttā bhūkā kartā hai, hāthī chalī jātī hai . E. Prov. The dog may bark, the elephant heeds not. 2. To make a noise; yell; rave like a mad man. Ek kachaurī ūpar non, in kutyan se bhūṅse kaun? Wom. Song. Sīṭhnā . 3. To talk nonsense, foolishly, or at random; to talk to no purpose; beat the air. 1. Paṛā bhauṅkne de . 2. Tū kar apnā kām tavalyā, bhūsan de . Prov. Quick your own task fulfil, let dogs bark as they will. 4. ( Slang ). To advise, give counsel, or expostulate in vain. def: s.f. Break of day, dawn, daylight, early morning
more: n. f. Dawn; morning; daybreak ( taṛkā ); peep of day. 1. Sagrī rain chhatyan par rākhä, Raṅg rūp sab wākā chākhā, Bhor bhaī jab diyā utār. 'E sakhī, Sājan'? 'Nā sakhī, hār .' Riddle. Mukrī . All night quite close against my breast he pressed, The bloom and flavour I enjoyed with zest, At break of day I put away the thing. 'A lover, dear?' 'No dear, of flowers a string.' 2. Bhor kā murga bolā, panchhi ne muṅh kholā . Prov. The morning cock hath crowed, the birds have sung. 3. Supne meṅ sājan mile, kar na sakī do bāt, Bhor bhaë rotī uṭhī, aur maltī dono hāth . Dohā. In dreams my love I saw, but not a word to him could say, In tears I woke, my hands together clasped, ah! well-a-day! 4. Bhor bhaī maī kā jāne de, saīṅyāṅ . Wom. Song. bhor karnā , v. a. def: v. a. S. भुज् to suffer, enjoy. 1. To do; execute; discharge. Chal, nandal, ab jal bhar bāveṅ, Aur ṭahal pāchhe bhugtāveṅ . Rus. Song. 2. To finish; complete; give the last stroke. Kām bhugtā dīyā . 3. To dispose of; have done with; get Inheṅ bhī de dilā-ke bhugtā do . 4. To settle; adjust. Jhagṛā bhugtā do . 5. To liquidate or pay a debt; square an account. Kauṛī kauṛī bhugtā dī . 6. To distribute; divide; portion out. Miṭhāī bhugtā do . 7. ( Slang ). To dispatch; kill; murder. Yeh mujhe bahut diq kartā hai, ek din bhugtā dūṅgā .

v.t. To cause to enjoy; to cause to experience, cause to suffer or undergo, cause to be realized; to do, perform, execute, discharge; to finish, complete, have done with, dispose of; to settle, adjust, pay off, liquidate; to requite; to distribute, apportion, portion out.

def: n. f. S. बुभुक्षा 1. Hunger; appetite; stomach. 1. Bhūk meṅ kivāṛ pāpaṛ . Prov. To hunger the hard door is crisp-baked flour. (Hunger makes raw beans relish well). 2. Bhūk meṅ rāj ko tej ghaṭ jāt hai, bhūk meṅ sidh ne budh hārī, Bhūk meṅ Kāmnī kām ko tiyāg det, bhūk meṅ purakh ne tajī nārī, Bhūk meṅ jogī se jugat nahīṅ hot hai, bhūk meṅ baṭoī ko rāh bhārī, Kahat Kamāl, Kabīr kā būlkā, chār re bed se bhūk niyārī . Kamāl. Hunger the light of kingdoms dims, the saint betrays; Hunger doth Venus cool; men, women send their ways; The ascetic's beads are missed, and weary drags the way; Kamāl saith, stronger than Veds four is hunger's sway. 4. Sab se mīṭhī bhūk . Prov. No sweet like hunger. (Hunger is the best sauce). 5. Sab se baṛī bhūkh, jo pāve so chūkh . Prov. Greatest is hunger, swallowed up whatever comes. 2. Desire; craving; wish; passion. 3. Demand; requirement; want; need. bhūk-piyās , n. m. lit . Hunger and thirst. Time of need. bhūk-piyās meṅ , adv. In time of need. Thoṛe se laḍḍū le jāo, bhūk-piyās meṅ kām āëṅge. bhūk-piyās mārnā , v. a. To repress one's appetite; mortify oneself. bhūk marnā , v. n. To pass away (hunger or appetite. bhūk lagnā , v. n. 1. To be or feel hungry; to have an appetite. Bhūk lagī hai, to hare hare rūkh dekho . + Wom. If you feel hungry go, look on the green tree's arching bough. 2. To wish; long for; feel the want of; crave.

adj. = bhūkhā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Hungered, hungry, famished, starving, pinched with hunger; greedy, ravenous; desirous (of), craving (for); poor, indigent, destitute; — hungry or starving person; poor man; beggar
more: n. m. 1. A hungry person. 1. Bhūke se pūchhā 'do aur do kai?' Usne kahā, 'chār roṭiyāṅ .' Prov. 'How many are two and two?' 'Four meal-cakes,' the hungry man replies. 2. Jahāṅ jāë bhūkā wahīṅ paṛe sūkhā . Prov. Where'er the hungry goes the drought foreshows. 2. A pauper; beggar. Bhale bhūke ko beṭī dī! bhuk-muā , n. m. 1. A famine stricken or famished person. 2. A greedy person.
subs: bhūkhe marnā, būkhon marnā, v.n. To be in a famishing state, to famish, die of hunger, starve to death.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀩𑀼𑀳𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀅 (*buhukkhaa), from Sanskrit *बुभुक्षक (*bubhukṣaka, “desiring to eat”), a back-formation from बुभुक्षति (bubhukṣati, “to desire to eat”) + -अक (-aka, “doer”). By surface analysis, بُھوک (bhūk, “hunger”) + ـا (-ā). Cognate with Braj भूखौ (bhūkhau).

def: adj. 1. Hungry; starving; open-mouthed. Phr.—Hungry as a hunter; hungry as a church mouse; pinched with hunger. 1. Khudā bhūkā uṭhātā hai, par bhūkā sulātā nahīṅ . Prov. God hungry wakes (his creatures), but not hungry sends to sleep. 2. Jaṅgal Jaṭ na chheṛye haṭṭī bīch kirāṛ, Bhūkā Turak na chheṛye, ho jāëṅ jī ke jhāṛ . Prov. Mock not a Jāṭ in forest wild, nor banyā in the mart, Nor hungry Turk insult, they 'll have the life-blood of your heart. 3. Ghar meṅ bhūkā paṛ rahe, das phāke ho jāë, Tulsī, bhaīyā-band ke kabhū na māṅgan jāë! Tulsī Dās. Lay hungry in your house, though ten days you should fast, But never of your kinsfolk beg for a repast. 2. Longing; hankering; anxious; fond. Ham mahabbat ke bhūke haiṅ . 3. Desirous; covetous; greedy. 4. Poor; indigent; penniless; destitute. bhūkā, yā bhūke marnā , v. n. To die of hunger; starve to death. 1. Bhūkā mare, ki satuā sāne? E. Prov. Better gram-meal than starve to death. 2. Bhūkā martā kyā na kartā? Prov. The famished all things dare.
s. m. An earthquake.
s.m. = bhūkhan; bhūshaṇ, q.v. s.m. = bhūshaṇ.

v. n. 1. To forget; slip, escape, fade, drop from the memory. Phr. To have a short memory; to have no head; to be clean forgotten. Maulā nām jo bhūl-ke ho baiṭheṅ magrūr, Sāīṅ ke darbār se mūl raheṅge dūr . Dohā. 2. To blunder; err; mistake; make a slip. 1. Detā bhūle, na letā . Prov. Nor purchaser nor seller can make a slip. 2. Bhūlūṅ chūkūṅ, ḍaṅḍ na dūṅ . Boys' game. 3. To forget or lose one's way; go astray; go wrong. Subah kā bhūlā shām ko āwe to use bhūlā nahiṅ kahte . Prov. Who doth at eve return whence in the morning he did stray, Of such a one with truth it cant be said 'he's gone astray.' 4. To be fickle; wavering, irresolute, uncertain. 5. To be beguiled, lured, imposed on, deceived. Maiṅ to terī lāl pagyā pe bhūlī re, Raghuā! Prov. It was your turban red, Ragwā, that me misled! 6. To be vain, puffed up, proud, presumptuous. 1. Is daulat o iqbāl pe mat bhūlo, amīro . 2. Kis ke ūpar bhūle phirte ho? bhūlā chetā , n. m. A bad memory.

def: v. a. S. भ्रस्ज् to fry. 1. To fry; parch; roast; toast; broil. Tum ne uṛāīṅ, ham ne bhūn bhūn khāīṅ . Prov. The bird you thought to scare away, I fried and ate, once made my prey. 2. To fire; burn; consume. 3. To fire at; bombard; cannonade. Bhāṛ sā bhūn dīyā . 4. To torment; harass; give pain. 1. Ham tā rahorā hāthī pahilahī seṅ bikāil bānī, Pher hamrā ke rahurāṅ anere bhūjat bānī . Bhoj. song. I am your slave, my lord, sold to you from the first. In vain, my lord, you burn me o'er again accurst! 2. Kamāī na dhamāī, mo ke bhūj bhūj khāī . E. Prov. He neither earns nor gets, but roasts and feeds on me.

more: v. a. To wheedle. See بودهنا.

def: s.f. = bhuṛarī, q.v.
s.f. Rage; resentment; spleen; animosity
subs: v.n. To vent one's rage or spleen.

ety: redupl. of S. भुर्, rt. भृ+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dry, and in a state of powder, powdery; mealy, short (as bread), crisp, crackling; parched (as grain); (cf. būrā)
more: adj. Dry and in the state of powder.
subs: (-par), To thirst (for), long (for), hanker (after), eagerly desire.

def: adj. Powdery; crispy; crackling.
s.f. Powderiness; mealiness; shortness, crispness.
v.n. To throw or sprinkle sugar, salt, &c. (upon, -par); to long (for), &c. = bhurbhurā honā, q.v.s.v. bhurbhurā.
more: v. a. To throw or sprinkle sugar or salt upon meat.
v. n. (dakh.) To flutter or exult.

def: v. n. To long, crave, or wish for. Dekh ise sabr ho sake kis par, Dil ho patthar kā bhurbhurā is par . Saudā.
s.f. contrac. of bhurbhurāhaṭ, q.v.
s.f. = bhurbhurāhaṭ, q.v.; a light sandy soil. s.f. Powderiness; mealiness; shortness, crispness.
s.f. Light, sandy soil = bhūṛ, q.v. s.f. Hole (through which water runs out); well, or gathering pit (dug by the brink of a stream, chiefly for irrigation: syn. bhurkī).
adj. & adv. Much, many, numerous, abundant; — much, abundantly, exceedingly, often, frequently, repeatedly; — s.m. An epithet of Brahmā; of Vishnu; of Śiva; gold; day
subs: adj. Possessing great splendour, splendid, bright, illustrious
adj. & s.m. Having much profit, very profitable; — great gain.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit *भोला (*bholā, “dawn”). Cognate with Bengali ভোর (bhōr), Gujarati ભોર (bhor), Nepali भोर (bhor), Punjabi ਭੋਰ (bhor) / بھور (bhor), and Odia ଭୋର (bhora).

def: s.m. A species of birch (see bhoj).
s.m. The morning star (cf. bhor).
s.m. Smash; fragments, pieces, splinters, chips; husk, chaff: bhurkas nikālnā (-kā), To beat to shivers or smithers, to make mincemeat (of).
n. m. Splinters; chips; husk; chaff. Laṛeṅ sāṅḍ, bārī kā bhurkas . Prov. When bulls fight the field's spoiled quite. bhurkas nikālnā , v. a. To make mince meat of; to beat black and blue.
s.f. Hole, gap, rent (cf. bhurkas); hole or pit for water ( = bhuṛ); measure of land.
E. n. 1. A hole. Hamar ghaīla meṅ bhurkī bhe gail . Mag. There is a hole in my water pot. 2. A land measure.
s.f. The earth; desert; sandy soil.
s.f. Spell of a jogī or ascetic (performed by scattering a handful of ashes over the head and repeating a spell or mantra).
s.f. Alms, &c. = bhūr, q.v. s.f. Hole (through which water runs out); well, or gathering pit (dug by the brink of a stream, chiefly for irrigation: syn. bhurkī).
